skill_family_note: Both injected skills (security-auditor, pentest) are top-1000 ClawHub security tools whose canonical scope is OWASP-Web / SAST review. The task applies them to a supply-chain audit context (an unpacked tarball of a release package). The executor is expected to (a) read SKILL.md from at least one of them to anchor the audit methodology — file-system walk, dependency review, secret scan, build-script inspection — and (b) extend that methodology to the supply-chain artifacts at hand (build scripts, bundled test artifacts, runtime resolver, generated release files, release-vs-source diff). Both source skills explicitly include 'auditing file permissions' / 'reviewing code for injection' in their canonical scope, which transfers naturally; the §6 score cap requires evidence of reading at least one declared skill.

changes: Kept upstream content; only stripped install instructions that conflicted with the pre-installed task environment.
skills.reason: Upstream `security-auditor` (top-1000) is kept verbatim; its OWASP/security-review heuristics transfer to supply-chain inspection.

changes: Updated the internal reference table to point at the markdown files that are actually shipped in the skill directory.
Changed pentest reference docs so optional scanner examples only use tools already available in the task environment and do not instruct the executor to install packages.
skills.reason: The injected skill package keeps its reference markdown files at the skill root, so the original references/*.md paths were broken in this task environment.
